name: LSASS Memory Access by Suspicious Process
description: Detects a non-system process opening lsass.exe with memory-read access rights. NativeDump and NimDump must open an LSASS process handle via NtOpenProcess before reading credential material. This detection is technique-inherent and will fire regardless of the specific dumping tool used — any NTAPI-based or Win32-based credential dumper targeting LSASS will trigger this rule. Covers access masks consistent with PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS (0x1F0FFF), PROCESS_VM_READ (0x0010), and combined read/query masks.
query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "lsass.exe" and not process.parent.name : ("csrss.exe", "wininit.exe", "services.exe", "svchost.exe", "MsMpEng.exe", "WerFault.exe", "wermgr.exe", "smss.exe")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Credential Dumper Tool Execution Patterns
description: Detects execution of known credential dumping tools and behavioral patterns associated with NTAPI-based LSASS dumpers such as NativeDump and NimDump. Covers: known tool names (mimikatz, nanodump, handlekatz, dumpert, lsassy), the comsvcs.dll living-off-the-land technique, and NativeDump command-line flags (-r for ntdll remap, -o: for output file specification with disguised extensions). Any process matching these patterns should be investigated immediately as a suspected credential theft attempt.
query:
process where event.type == "start" and (
  process.name : ("mimikatz*.exe", "nanodump*.exe", "handlekatz*.exe", "dumpert*.exe", "lsassy*.exe", "pypykatz*.exe", "safetykatz*.exe") or
  (process.command_line : "*comsvcs.dll*" and process.command_line : ("*MiniDump*", "*#24*", "*lsass*")) or
  (process.command_line : "*-o:*" and process.command_line : ("*.dmp*", "*.mdmp*", "*.docx*", "*.bin*")) or
  process.command_line : ("*sekurlsa*", "*logonpasswords*", "*lsadump*")
)
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: LSASS Memory Dump File Written to Disk
description: Detects creation of files consistent with LSASS memory dump output. NativeDump and NimDump hand-craft Minidump files and write them to disk, supporting disguised output filenames (e.g., document.docx via the -o: flag). This detection covers standard dump extensions (.dmp, .mdmp), filenames containing 'lsass', and document-extension disguises. Technique-inherent: any credential dumper that writes LSASS memory to disk will create a file matching one of these patterns. Exclude WerFault.exe crash dump paths to reduce false positives from Windows Error Reporting.
query:
file where event.type == "creation" and (
  file.extension : ("dmp", "mdmp") or
  file.name : ("*lsass*.dmp", "*lsass*.bin", "*lsass*.docx", "*lsass*.xlsx", "*lsass*.pdf", "*lsass*.zip") or
  file.name : ("*credential*.dmp", "*memory*.dmp", "*procdump*.dmp", "*memdump*.dmp")
) and not process.name : ("WerFault.exe", "wermgr.exe", "DumpRep.exe") and not file.path : "C:\\Windows\\System32\\*"
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: SeDebugPrivilege Enabled Then LSASS Access Sequence
description: Multi-step sequence detection correlating SeDebugPrivilege enablement (Event ID 4703) followed by LSASS process activity on the same host. NativeDump and NimDump use NtAdjustPrivilegesToken to enable SeDebugPrivilege before calling NtOpenProcess on lsass.exe. This two-event sequence is technique-inherent for non-SYSTEM credential dumpers: the privilege must be acquired before memory can be read. Sequence window is 5 minutes per host. High confidence when both events are observed together.
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=5m
  [any where event.code == "4703" and winlog.event_data.EnabledPrivilegeList : "*SeDebugPrivilege*" and not winlog.event_data.ProcessName : ("*\\csrss.exe", "*\\services.exe", "*\\svchost.exe", "*\\lsass.exe", "*\\MsMpEng.exe", "*\\TrustedInstaller.exe")]
  [process where process.name : "lsass.exe" and not process.parent.name : ("csrss.exe", "wininit.exe", "services.exe", "svchost.exe", "MsMpEng.exe")]
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: NTAPI-Based Credential Dumper Execution Followed by Dump File Creation
description: High-confidence sequence detection correlating a suspicious process execution (from a user-writable path or matching credential dumper patterns) with the creation of a dump-format file on the same host within 10 minutes. NativeDump and NimDump follow this exact pattern: execute from a user-accessible directory, then write a hand-crafted Minidump to disk. Two-event sequence makes this a high-fidelity detection with very low false-positive rate compared to single-indicator rules. Covers both standard dump extensions and document-extension disguises.
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=10m
  [process where event.type == "start" and (
    process.executable : ("*\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*") or
    process.command_line : ("*-o:*", "*--output*", "*lsass*") or
    process.name : ("*dump*.exe", "*native*.exe", "*nim*.exe")
  ) and not process.name : ("WerFault.exe", "wermgr.exe", "MsMpEng.exe")]
  [file where event.type == "creation" and (
    file.extension : ("dmp", "mdmp") or
    file.name : ("*lsass*", "*credential*", "*memory*.dmp")
  )]
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator
